articles.title: Mass schedule, reconciliation hours, and sacramental preparation
articles.content: Daily and Sunday Mass are celebrated in the university chapel; the full schedule, including holy days, is posted each term. Reconciliation is offered weekly and by appointment. Students preparing for Baptism, Confirmation, or First Communion can join the sacramental-preparation track through Campus Ministry. All are welcome to attend liturgies regardless of faith background.
articles.snippet: Daily and Sunday Mass are celebrated in the university chapel; the full schedule, including holy days, is posted each term

articles.title: Going on retreat: Silent, Magis, and Ignatian Spiritual Exercises options
articles.content: Campus Ministry offers a range of retreats: short weekend Silent retreats, the community-building Magis retreat, and the 19th-Annotation Ignatian Spiritual Exercises spread across a semester. Retreats are open to students of all faiths and none and are a space for reflection, rest, and discernment. Spots fill quickly; register early and reach out if cost is a barrier — scholarships are available.
articles.snippet: Campus Ministry offers a range of retreats: short weekend Silent retreats, the community-building Magis retreat, and the 19th-Annotation Ignatian Spiritual E...

articles.title: Talking to a chaplain — pastoral counseling vs. spiritual direction
articles.content: A chaplain offers two distinct kinds of accompaniment. Pastoral counseling is short-term support around a specific struggle — grief, a hard transition, a crisis of meaning. Spiritual direction is an ongoing relationship that helps you notice God's movement in your life over time. Neither replaces clinical mental-health care; if you are in distress, we will walk with you and connect you to Counseling Services. Conversations with a chaplain are confidential and open to everyone.
articles.snippet: A chaplain offers two distinct kinds of accompaniment

articles.title: Service & immersion programs and how to apply
articles.content: From weekly local service to domestic and international immersion trips, Campus Ministry helps students live 'for and with others.' Applications open each semester and include a short reflection and an interest meeting. We also advise students discerning a year of service after graduation (JVC, Amate, and similar programs). Contact us to talk through options and deadlines.
articles.snippet: From weekly local service to domestic and international immersion trips, Campus Ministry helps students live 'for and with others.' Applications open each se...

articles.title: Interfaith programming, prayer spaces, and dietary observances
articles.content: Campus Ministry serves students of all faiths and none. The campus offers a multifaith prayer and meditation space, ablution facilities, and partnerships with local religious communities. We coordinate halal, kosher, and fasting-friendly dining options with Dining Services and host interfaith dialogue and holy-day observances throughout the year. Tell us what you need and we will help arrange it.
articles.snippet: Campus Ministry serves students of all faiths and none

articles.title: Discernment groups for students considering religious or service vocations
articles.content: Discernment groups gather students who are weighing big life questions — a year of service, graduate school, a career choice, or a religious vocation — in a reflective, Ignatian setting. Groups meet regularly with a chaplain facilitator and pair well with 1:1 spiritual direction. There is no commitment implied by joining; the goal is freedom and clarity. Reach out to Campus Ministry to be matched with a group or a director.
